sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.74 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a non-elementary hyperbolic group and let$G^n$be the subgroup generated by the$n$th powers of the elements of$G$. (M.\,Gromov). Is it true that$G/G^n$is infinite for some$n = n(G)$? Is it true that$_{n=1}^{ } G^n=\{ 1\}$? { A.\,Yu.\,Olshanskii} a) Yes, it is; b) Yes, it is (S.\,V.\,Ivanov, A.\,Yu.\,Olshanskii, Trans. Amer. Math. Soc., { 348}, N 6 (1996), 2091--2138).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
